sections.heading: Deep Dive: Reported Speech
sections.body: Let's go deeper into Reported Speech. This section covers the internals and advanced concepts that separate beginners from proficient developers.

code.explanation: This example shows the basic structure. In practice, you'll use Reported Speech throughout your projects.

sections.heading: Common Patterns & Best Practices
sections.body:
Here are the best practices for Reported Speech:

1. Start Simple: Begin with the simplest implementation that works, then add complexity as needed.

2. Follow Conventions: Stick to established patterns and conventions in your codebase. Consistency makes code maintainable.

3. Test Your Work: Always test your implementation. Write tests that cover both happy paths and edge cases.

4. Document Decisions: Add comments that explain WHY, not WHAT. Future you (and teammates) will thank you.

5. Review and Refactor: Code reviews and regular refactoring keep code quality high.

sections.heading: Practice Task
sections.body: Apply what you've learned about Reported Speech. Try building a small project that uses these concepts. Start with the basics and gradually add features.

summary: Reported Speech is an essential concept that you'll use throughout your development journey. Direct vs indirect speech and tense changes. Practice the examples in this lesson and apply them to your own projects.
